title: CLSK Q2 Earnings Miss: Stock Slides Amid Financial Challenges
text: CleanSpark Inc disappointed investors with Q2 results that fell short on both revenue and profitability. The company reported revenue of $136.4M versus the expected $145.4M consensus. More significantly, the loss per share came in at $1.52 compared to the forecasted $0.56 loss, a substantial earnings shortfall. The underlying financials paint a challenging picture. CleanSpark operates with severe margin pressures, showing an EBIT margin around -46.7% and profit margin near -53%. The company is burning cash at an alarming rate, with free cash flow at approximately -$198.1M during the recent period. Despite these headwinds, the balance sheet offers some cushion with roughly $458.1M in cash reserves against $1.79B in total debt. Following the earnings announcement, CLSK stock declined approximately 12.76%. Intraday price action revealed typical post-earnings volatility—the stock initially spiked to $14.34 before fading to close near $12.48. Earnings are catalysts, not guarantees. The edge comes from reacting to the price action, not predicting the news. The stock's wide trading range and volatile swings present both risks and opportunities for active traders who employ disciplined risk management.
